content.full_text:
Oct 1 9 07 4M 90

GRAND JURY
COUNTY GOVERNMENT CENTER
SAN LUIS OBISPO, CALIFORNIA 93408

September 24, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Reference: Form 730, Statement of Economic Interests For Designated Employees

Gentlemen:

We are working on revisions for our Grand Jury handbook and want it as accurate as possible.

Are there any circumstances under which a Grand Juror is not required to file a Conflict of Interest form 730? If so, what are these circumstances?

Should Grand Jurors be treated as trial jurors?

Your Form 730 instructions include a definition of a "designated employee". Do Grand Jurors fall within this definition? The case could be made that Grand Jurors only investigate and recommend and do not make decisions.

Please respond with supporting citations and authorities. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

William F. Newberry
Foreman

sections.question: Are there any circumstances under which a Grand Juror is not required to file a Conflict of Interest form 730? If so, what are these circumstances? Should Grand Jurors be treated as trial jurors? Your Form 730 instructions include a definition of a "designated employee". Do Grand Jurors fall within this definition? The case could be made that Grand Jurors only investigate and recommend and do not make decisions.
sections.question_synthetic: The San Luis Obispo County Grand Jury seeks clarification on whether grand jurors are required to file Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) and whether they fall within the definition of "designated employees" under FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. The document contains multiple related questions about grand juror conflict of interest disclosure requirements.
